title: MODIFIED SLING PROCEDURE FOR TREATMENT OF UTEROVAGINAL PROLAPSE
author: Dr. Sania Karim, Dr Khalid Hussain, Dr Muhammad Hussnain Idrees
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Objectives   : To evaluate the results of modified sling procedure performed for the treatment of uterovaginal prolapse.  

  Method: A case series was studied in 20 patients wishing to preserve their fertilityat Sheikh Zayed hospital Rahim Yar Khan from January 2016 to December 2016.  

   Results:    All 20 patients were successfully operated only one patient developed fever in postoperative period and one patient developed recurrence of prolapse. There was no major intraoperative and postoperative complication.  

  Conclusion:  An easier procedure with negligible blood loss, less complications, less time consuming and good results. Residents can also perform it.
